story.title: Sea turtles don’t just swim—they fly underwater 🐢
story.description: A clip of reptiles: sea turtles swimming in sea life sanctuary
story.seo_title: Sea turtles don’t just swim—they fly underwater 🐢
story.yt_description: Sea turtles. These reptiles don’t just paddle—they fly. Their flippers move in a figure-eight motion like a bird’s wings. Source: Pexels
story.script: Sea turtles fly underwater—not swim. Watch the front flippers: they move in slow figure-eights, like a bird’s wings. Their rear legs steer, but they’re basically underwater aviators. That’s why they’re so fast and graceful. Ever seen one in person? Comment below.
story.lead: Sea turtles fly underwater—not swim. Watch the front flippers: they move in slow figure-eights, like a bird’s wings. Their rear legs steer, but they’re basically underwater aviators. That’s why they’re so fast and graceful. Ever seen one in person? Comment below.

story.title: Sea turtles can sleep for hours underwater
story.description: A clip of reptiles: serene underwater turtle gliding over coral reef
story.seo_title: Sea turtles can sleep for hours underwater
story.yt_description: Sea turtles. These ocean wanderers can snooze underwater for hours at a stretch. Source: Pexels
story.hook: A sea turtle can nap underwater.
story.script: A sea turtle can nap underwater. I love this detail—watch their gills pulse slow and steady. They hold their breath for hours, then surface just enough to refill. That’s why they can sleep this long without waking. Ever see one doze off? Comment your clip below.

story.title: Hedgehogs roll like tank treads 🦔
story.description: A clip of nocturnal animals: close up of hedgehog in garden foliage
story.yt_description: A hedgehog in the garden. Hedgehogs' spines aren't just for show—they’re a tank-like defense. Source: Pexels
story.script: Hedgehogs roll like tank treads. Those spines. They’re hollow, lightweight, and packed with muscle. Watch the feet scramble as it tucks in tight. That’s why a predator sees nothing but spikes. Ever seen one do this in real life.
